brief: A drift-watch dashboard for the open-weight model fleet we serve in production: every model plotted by population stability index against its breach limit, the one breaching model flagged with its trend history and feature-level drivers, and the overnight incident log.

arguments.contentBrief: A drift-watch dashboard for the open-weight model fleet we serve in production: every model plotted by population stability index against its breach limit, the one breaching model flagged with its trend history and feature-level drivers, and the overnight incident log.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'cockpit' (db-model-monitoring-cockpit, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [technical]; matched intent keyword(s) [monitor, model, health, detect, drift, early, fleet]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 1 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The dealing-floor chrome — command line, environment, the watch clock start, and the required provenance notice.

spec.name: watch.pageTitle
spec.guidance: The browser-tab title stem (the template appends " — Live"), e.g. "The Model Risk Control Room, 02:47".

slots.guidance: The browser-tab title stem (the template appends " — Live"), e.g. "The Model Risk Control Room, 02:47".

spec.name: watch.commandLine
spec.guidance: The command-room line on the top chrome, e.g. "MODEL RISK COMMAND · NIGHT WATCH · EST. 2021".

spec.guidance: The last-sweep stamp in the footer, e.g. "LAST SWEEP 02:47:12".

sections.purpose: The hero narrative — the kicker, the multi-line display statement, and the one-line breach subline.

spec.name: statement.kicker
spec.guidance: The hero eyebrow above the statement, e.g. "THE MODEL RISK CONTROL ROOM · 02:47".

slots.guidance: The hero eyebrow above the statement, e.g. "THE MODEL RISK CONTROL ROOM · 02:47".

spec.name: statement.lines
spec.guidance: Two-to-four display lines (one per array entry) building to the flagged model, e.g. "The twelfth is why this room is lit.".

slots.guidance: Two-to-four display lines (one per array entry) building to the flagged model, e.g. "The twelfth is why this room is lit.".
slots.example: The twelfth is why this room is lit.

spec.name: statement.subline
spec.guidance: One-line reading of the breach under the statement, e.g. "CARD-FRAUD-V4 CROSSED PSI 0.250 AT 09:41 THU · 41 H IN BREACH · RETRAIN QUEUED".

slots.guidance: One-line reading of the breach under the statement, e.g. "CARD-FRAUD-V4 CROSSED PSI 0.250 AT 09:41 THU · 41 H IN BREACH · RETRAIN QUEUED".

sections.purpose: The drift-scope instrument — the drift thresholds it rings, its accessible caption and encoding legend.

spec.name: thresholds.watch
spec.guidance: The watch-band PSI threshold (the inner ring), on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale and strictly below thresholds.breach, e.g. 0.1.

slots.guidance: The watch-band PSI threshold (the inner ring), on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale and strictly below thresholds.breach, e.g. 0.1.

spec.name: thresholds.breach
spec.guidance: The breach-limit PSI threshold (the outer ring), on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale and strictly above thresholds.watch, e.g. 0.25.

slots.guidance: The breach-limit PSI threshold (the outer ring), on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale and strictly above thresholds.watch, e.g. 0.25.

spec.name: scope.caption
spec.guidance: The full accessible caption of the aria-hidden scope, naming the breaching model and pointing to the watchlist table, e.g. "Fleet drift scope: twelve production models plotted by 30-day population stability index …".

slots.guidance: The full accessible caption of the aria-hidden scope, naming the breaching model and pointing to the watchlist table, e.g. "Fleet drift scope: twelve production models plotted by 30-day population stability index …".
slots.example: Fleet drift scope: twelve production models plotted by 30-day population stability index …

spec.name: scope.encodingNote
spec.guidance: How to read the instrument — its encoding legend line, e.g. "RADIUS = 30-DAY PSI · SECTOR = BUSINESS LINE".

slots.guidance: How to read the instrument — its encoding legend line, e.g. "RADIUS = 30-DAY PSI · SECTOR = BUSINESS LINE".

spec.name: scope.breachCalloutNote
spec.guidance: The breach callout continuation under the derived PSI/limit row, e.g. "41 H IN BREACH · RETRAIN QUEUED".

slots.guidance: The breach callout continuation under the derived PSI/limit row, e.g. "41 H IN BREACH · RETRAIN QUEUED".

sections.purpose: The fleet watchlist — the scope's textual mirror; exactly one model is flagged in breach.

spec.name: fleet.bandTitle
spec.guidance: The band heading for the watchlist, e.g. "FLEET WATCHLIST".

slots.guidance: The band heading for the watchlist, e.g. "FLEET WATCHLIST".

spec.name: fleet.bandSub
spec.guidance: The band sub-line, true of the fleet you supply, e.g. "TEXTUAL MIRROR OF THE SCOPE · 12 CONTACTS".

slots.guidance: The band sub-line, true of the fleet you supply, e.g. "TEXTUAL MIRROR OF THE SCOPE · 12 CONTACTS".

spec.name: fleet.tableCaption
spec.guidance: The accessible caption of the watchlist table naming its columns, e.g. "Fleet watchlist — the drift scope's contents as a table. Twelve models with sector, 30-day PSI, limit, status, owner, and last retrain date.".

slots.guidance: The accessible caption of the watchlist table naming its columns, e.g. "Fleet watchlist — the drift scope's contents as a table. Twelve models with sector, 30-day PSI, limit, status, owner, and last retrain date.".

spec.name: fleet.sectors
spec.guidance: Two-to-six business-line sectors (id, label); each becomes a quadrant of the scope, e.g. { id: "fraud", label: "FRAUD & FIN-CRIME" }.

slots.guidance: Two-to-six business-line sectors (id, label); each becomes a quadrant of the scope, e.g. { id: "fraud", label: "FRAUD & FIN-CRIME" }.

spec.name: fleet.models
spec.guidance: Six-to-sixteen models (id, name, sectorId, psi, status stable|watch|breach, owner, lastRetrain). psi is on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale. Exactly ONE carries status "breach" — the single flagged contact past its limit, e.g. card-fraud-v4 at psi 0.312.

slots.guidance: Six-to-sixteen models (id, name, sectorId, psi, status stable|watch|breach, owner, lastRetrain). psi is on the fixed 0–0.34 instrument scale. Exactly ONE carries status "breach" — the single flagged contact past its limit, e.g. card-fraud-v4 at psi 0.312.

sections.purpose: The breaching model's dossier — why the scope is pointing at it: drift trend, feature contributions, and register entry.

spec.name: dossier.bandTitle
spec.guidance: The dossier band heading stem; the breaching model name is appended, e.g. "THE DOSSIER".

slots.guidance: The dossier band heading stem; the breaching model name is appended, e.g. "THE DOSSIER".

spec.name: dossier.bandSub
spec.guidance: The dossier band sub-line stem; the breach limit is appended, e.g. "WHY THE SCOPE IS POINTING AT IT".

slots.guidance: The dossier band sub-line stem; the breach limit is appended, e.g. "WHY THE SCOPE IS POINTING AT IT".
slots.example: WHY THE SCOPE IS POINTING AT IT

spec.name: dossier.trendHeading
spec.guidance: The drift-trend panel heading, e.g. "DRIFT · 90 DAYS VS LIMIT".

slots.guidance: The drift-trend panel heading, e.g. "DRIFT · 90 DAYS VS LIMIT".

spec.name: dossier.trendChartTitle
spec.guidance: The trend chart's accessible title, e.g. "card-fraud-v4 — population stability index, 90 days".

slots.guidance: The trend chart's accessible title, e.g. "card-fraud-v4 — population stability index, 90 days".

spec.name: dossier.trendChartSource
spec.guidance: The trend chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "Daily 30-day-window PSI vs the 0.25 breach limit. Synthetic demonstration data.".

slots.guidance: The trend chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "Daily 30-day-window PSI vs the 0.25 breach limit. Synthetic demonstration data.".
slots.example: Daily 30-day-window PSI vs the 0.25 breach limit. Synthetic demonstration data.

spec.name: dossier.trendPoints
spec.guidance: Daily 30-day-window PSI points (x ISO date, y PSI on the fixed 0–0.35 trend axis) for the breaching model — the drift history the trend line draws.

slots.guidance: Daily 30-day-window PSI points (x ISO date, y PSI on the fixed 0–0.35 trend axis) for the breaching model — the drift history the trend line draws.

spec.name: dossier.featureHeading
spec.guidance: The feature-drift panel heading, e.g. "FEATURE CONTRIBUTIONS TO DRIFT".

slots.guidance: The feature-drift panel heading, e.g. "FEATURE CONTRIBUTIONS TO DRIFT".

spec.name: dossier.featureChartTitle
spec.guidance: The feature-bar chart's accessible title, e.g. "card-fraud-v4 — feature-level PSI contribution".

slots.guidance: The feature-bar chart's accessible title, e.g. "card-fraud-v4 — feature-level PSI contribution".

spec.name: dossier.featureChartSource
spec.guidance: The feature-bar source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "Top six features by contribution to composite PSI. Synthetic demonstration data.".

slots.guidance: The feature-bar source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "Top six features by contribution to composite PSI. Synthetic demonstration data.".
slots.example: Top six features by contribution to composite PSI. Synthetic demonstration data.

spec.name: dossier.featureDrift
spec.guidance: Three-to-eight features by contribution to composite PSI (id, category, value), e.g. { category: "txn_amount_zscore", value: 0.084 }.

slots.guidance: Three-to-eight features by contribution to composite PSI (id, category, value), e.g. { category: "txn_amount_zscore", value: 0.084 }.

spec.name: dossier.registerHeading
spec.guidance: The register-entry panel heading, e.g. "REGISTER ENTRY".

slots.guidance: The register-entry panel heading, e.g. "REGISTER ENTRY".

spec.name: dossier.facts
spec.guidance: Five-to-twelve register facts (label, value) about the breaching model, e.g. { label: "SLA", value: "RESTORE ≤ 72 H · 31 H REMAINING" }.

slots.guidance: Five-to-twelve register facts (label, value) about the breaching model, e.g. { label: "SLA", value: "RESTORE ≤ 72 H · 31 H REMAINING" }.

sections.purpose: The overnight event log — the incident feed of the night watch (comp.status-list).

spec.name: log.heading
spec.guidance: The log panel heading, e.g. "OVERNIGHT LOG".

slots.guidance: The log panel heading, e.g. "OVERNIGHT LOG".

spec.name: log.listTitle
spec.guidance: The status-list accessible title, e.g. "Overnight event log".

slots.guidance: The status-list accessible title, e.g. "Overnight event log".

spec.name: log.items
spec.guidance: Three-to-eight overnight events (id, label, status success|warning|danger|info|neutral, description, ISO timestamp), newest first.

slots.guidance: Three-to-eight overnight events (id, label, status success|warning|danger|info|neutral, description, ISO timestamp), newest first.

sections.purpose: The supporting instrumentation — fleet-level gauges subordinate to the scope (comp.kpi-tile).

spec.name: instruments.bandTitle
spec.guidance: The instruments band heading, e.g. "SUPPORTING INSTRUMENTATION".

slots.guidance: The instruments band heading, e.g. "SUPPORTING INSTRUMENTATION".

spec.name: instruments.bandSub
spec.guidance: The instruments band sub-line, e.g. "FLEET-LEVEL GAUGES · SUBORDINATE TO THE SCOPE".

slots.guidance: The instruments band sub-line, e.g. "FLEET-LEVEL GAUGES · SUBORDINATE TO THE SCOPE".
slots.example: FLEET-LEVEL GAUGES · SUBORDINATE TO THE SCOPE

spec.name: instruments.kpiTitle
spec.guidance: The KPI tile's accessible title, e.g. "Fleet gauges".

slots.guidance: The KPI tile's accessible title, e.g. "Fleet gauges".

spec.name: instruments.kpis
spec.guidance: Three-to-six fleet gauges (label, value, unit, status). Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.992 renders as 99.2%, never 99.

slots.guidance: Three-to-six fleet gauges (label, value, unit, status). Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.992 renders as 99.2%, never 99.

craftGuarantees: A model-risk night watch staged as a DEALING-FLOOR INSTRUMENT: the commanding visual is a radial DRIFT SCOPE — every production model is a contact placed at its 30-day-PSI radius inside its business-line sector, with threshold rings at the watch and breach limits and a slow phosphor sweep that holds on demand and never renders under reduced motion.

Exactly one fleet model carries the "breach" status: the single contact past the limit ring, marked with a crosshair + halo and a callout, echoed as the breach row of the watchlist and as the climax of the hero statement.
The synthetic-data provenance notice (watch.dataNotice) is required and prints in the hero and the footer.
The scope is decorative (aria-hidden); the REAL content is the fleet watchlist table — the scope's textual mirror — so the world is fully legible without the instrument.
The dossier auto-titles the breaching model and derives its breach callout, trend series labels, and ring labels from the fleet + thresholds, so the diagnosis always names whichever model is flagged.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the statement never overflows, the watchlist and scope stay balanced, and the dossier panels stay legible. Every PSI value is bounded to the instrument's fixed scale (contacts and thresholds 0–0.34, trend points 0–0.35, watch strictly below breach), so no fill can pin a contact to the rim or clip the trend.
Motion level 1 (data-ink-draw + phosphor sweep); the mood is locked dark.

spec.name: instruments.kpis
spec.guidance: Three-to-six fleet gauges (label, value, unit, status). Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.992 renders as 99.2%, never 99.

slots.guidance: Three-to-six fleet gauges (label, value, unit, status). Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.992 renders as 99.2%, never 99.

craftGuarantees: A model-risk night watch staged as a DEALING-FLOOR INSTRUMENT: the commanding visual is a radial DRIFT SCOPE — every production model is a contact placed at its 30-day-PSI radius inside its business-line sector, with threshold rings at the watch and breach limits and a slow phosphor sweep that holds on demand and never renders under reduced motion.

Exactly one fleet model carries the "breach" status: the single contact past the limit ring, marked with a crosshair + halo and a callout, echoed as the breach row of the watchlist and as the climax of the hero statement.
The synthetic-data provenance notice (watch.dataNotice) is required and prints in the hero and the footer.
The scope is decorative (aria-hidden); the REAL content is the fleet watchlist table — the scope's textual mirror — so the world is fully legible without the instrument.
The dossier auto-titles the breaching model and derives its breach callout, trend series labels, and ring labels from the fleet + thresholds, so the diagnosis always names whichever model is flagged.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the statement never overflows, the watchlist and scope stay balanced, and the dossier panels stay legible. Every PSI value is bounded to the instrument's fixed scale (contacts and thresholds 0–0.34, trend points 0–0.35, watch strictly below breach), so no fill can pin a contact to the rim or clip the trend.
Motion level 1 (data-ink-draw + phosphor sweep); the mood is locked dark.
